name: Cherry Belle
image_alt: Round bright red radishes with white flesh
description: The classic round red spring radish — bright scarlet skin, pure white flesh, and a crisp, mildly spicy bite. Matures in an astonishing 22 days from seeding.
history: An AAS winner in 1949, Cherry Belle became the standard small round spring radish for American home gardens. Its consistency, speed, and mild flavor made it the benchmark against which other spring radishes are measured.

good.reason: Radishes loosen soil and mark slow carrot rows

seasonal_quirks: Bolts to seed rapidly in heat — strictly a cool-season crop. In warm climates, grows only in early spring and fall. Sow in succession every 1–2 weeks for continuous supply. Over-mature roots become pithy and overly hot.
harvest_cues: Shoulders visible at soil surface, 1 inch in diameter. Pull one to check before harvesting the row. Delay makes roots pithy.
variety_notes: The speed of 22 days makes it uniquely useful as a row marker for slow-germinating crops like carrots and parsnips — sow together and the radishes emerge first, marking the row.
preservation: Quick pickle in rice vinegar with salt — ready in 30 minutes. Refrigerator pickles last 2 weeks.
